definition: The swappable policy engine that resolves a guard's predicate — CustomGuardProvider.evaluate(region, event, context): Promise<GuardDecision>. Resolved through the injector chain (default platform provider → project override → custom plug); the provider contract is the protocol's only lock. Unlike the synchronous, client-side positioning provider it shares a pattern with, a guard provider crosses a trust boundary: it is async, server-authoritative (the front-end decision is a UX mirror, never enforcement), and revocable (an optional subscribe() invalidation signal). The forbid(403)/cloak(404) disclosure decision lives here, surfaced as GuardDecision.reason — not in the UX-only member intent.
usage: Held in CustomGuardRegistry (window.customGuards); the project selects/overrides it via the platform config it extends (flavors), the tool carrying no default. Standardized by the Guard protocol (Web Guards).
